ops.title: Session stamping maintains wire format compatibility via optional field
ops.body:
Session ids are stamped conditionally to preserve a byte-identical wire format for session-less packs: `packWire.Session` carries `omitempty`, so a pack written with no session marshals exactly as the pre-session v1 format — entity ids (which hash the root pack bytes) and all existing golden vectors stay unchanged, and old binaries decode new packs by silently ignoring the key (plain json.Unmarshal, no packVersion bump; bumping the version IS the compat break to avoid).

Env precedence (resolved in `internal/store.session()`, stamped in all four writers Create/Append/Compact/Merge in write.go): `CC_NOTES_SESSION_ID` when set — set-but-empty is an explicit opt-out that suppresses stamping — else `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (set by Claude Code itself), else no stamp. This mirrors the `CC_NOTES_ACTOR` override pattern.

Never emit `"session":""`. Test hermeticity depends on `gittest.ScrubEnv` and the cli e2e `gitEnvKeys` scrubbing both vars — a test fixture that writes packs in-process without ScrubEnv leaks the live Claude session id into fixture bytes. Landed in commit 978a7ca913fa.
ops.when: When modifying pack format, session field handling, or environment variable precedence for session stamping
